general.description: Commit messages must follow Conventional Commits format in English. E.g.: feat(auth): add SSO login
Branches must use prefix: feat/, fix/, chore/, docs/, refactor/ followed by English description
No API keys, passwords, or tokens may be committed. Use environment variables only
Variables, functions, and classes must have readable names without abbreviations. Names must reveal intent
Commented-out code must be removed before merge. Comments only to explain why, never what
PRs must reference the Trello card in the title. E.g.: feat(auth): SSO login [ORFEU-42]
All code (.ts, .md), variables, functions, comments, file names, branches, and commit messages must be written in English. Documentation in Confluence and Trello cards may be in Portuguese

frontend.description: The any type is forbidden. Use explicit typing or unknown when the type is unknown
Inline styles forbidden. Use CSS classes, Tailwind, or design tokens
Components must not exceed 200 lines. Decompose into smaller components
Every interactive element must have aria-label, alt, and correct HTML semantics
User-visible text must be externalized via i18n, never hardcoded in the component
Global state only via the project-defined solution. Local state stays in the component
Every component must work on mobile (375px) and desktop (1280px+)

backend.description: Every business rule in service/ or domain/ must have unit tests. Minimum 80% coverage
Controllers only receive/respond to requests. Business logic exclusively in services/domain
Every API input must be validated via DTOs with explicit schemas (Zod, class-validator)
All exceptions must use the project's standard error class. Never throw generic Error
Raw SQL forbidden outside repositories. Use ORM or query builder (Prisma, Drizzle, Knex)
console.log forbidden in backend. Use the project's structured logger with info/warn/error levels
Endpoints must be versioned: /api/v1/... Breaking changes increment the version
